food_subgroup: Hot coffee beverages with plant-based milk

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for oat milk kopi. FOODON_03301339 (coffee beverage) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Coffee, with milk) is the closest parent; no specific oat milk variant code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 33479001 (Coffee substance) used as closest parent; no specific oat milk kopi concept exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of oat milk kopi as served at a Singapore kopitiam or café, approximately 250 ml, containing brewed robusta coffee blended with oat milk.

nutrient_source.title: Oat milk (fortified, unsweetened) and brewed coffee — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; estimated composite for oat milk kopi beverage
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Oat Milk Kopi exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from a composite of USDA FoodData Central entries: brewed coffee (FDC ID 171890) and oat milk unsweetened (FDC ID 719016), blended in an approximate 60:40 ratio (coffee:oat milk) typical of a Singapore-style kopi preparation. Sugar contribution is included at a moderate level (~5 g per 100 ml) reflecting typical kopitiam sweetening. All values are per 100 g of the final beverage.

gi_source.title: Oat milk glycemic index — analogous estimate based on oat milk GI data; Wolever TM et al., peer-reviewed literature on oat-based beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Oat Milk Kopi. GI estimated at 39 based on oat milk GI values reported in the University of Sydney GI Database (oat milk GI range 30–44, mean ~39) and peer-reviewed studies on oat-based beverages. The coffee component contributes negligible glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (39 × 16.25) / 100 ≈ 6.3 for a 250 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~70 mg per 250 ml serving); caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Oat milk kopi contains moderate sodium (~70 mg per serving) and caffeine from robusta coffee; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one cup per day and request unsweetened or lightly sweetened preparation to avoid excess caloric and sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from kopitiam-style sweetening raises glycemic load; oat milk contributes beta-glucan which may moderate postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request oat milk kopi kosong (unsweetened) or with minimal sugar, as the standard sweetened preparation contributes approximately 12 g of sugar per 250 ml serving; the oat milk component provides beta-glucan which may modestly attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat; oat milk beta-glucan may support LDL cholesterol reduction; caffeine intake should be moderated
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Oat milk kopi is low in saturated fat and cholesterol-free, and the beta-glucan in oat milk may contribute to modest LDL cholesterol reduction; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose unsweetened preparations and limit to one to two cups daily to avoid excess caffeine and added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Oat Milk Kopi is a modern Singaporean beverage that combines traditional Nanyang-style robusta coffee (kopi) with oat milk as a dairy-free alternative to the conventional condensed milk or evaporated milk used in classic kopitiam coffee. The drink is prepared by brewing dark-roasted robusta coffee through a traditional sock filter and blending it with oat milk, which imparts a mild, slightly sweet, and creamy texture with a subtle oat flavour. It is typically served hot or iced and may be sweetened with sugar or served kosong (without sugar). Oat Milk Kopi has gained popularity in Singapore as a plant-based, lactose-free option that retains the bold, slightly bitter character of traditional kopi while offering a lower saturated fat profile compared to condensed milk versions. Nutritionally, it provides moderate carbohydrates primarily from oat milk and added sugar, low protein, and low fat per serving. The oat milk component contributes beta-glucan, a soluble fibre associated with modest LDL cholesterol reduction and improved glycaemic response. The glycaemic index is estimated at 39 (low GI), making it a more favourable choice for blood sugar management compared to traditional sweetened kopi. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request the unsweetened version, while those with hypertension should be mindful of caffeine content. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those with lactose intolerance, though it is not gluten-free due to oat content.
common_user_queries: How many calories in oat milk kopi?
Is oat milk kopi suitable for diabetics?
Is oat milk kopi gluten free?
What is the difference between oat milk kopi and regular kopi?
Does oat milk kopi have less sugar than normal kopi?
Can I drink oat milk kopi if I am lactose intolerant?
What is the GI of oat milk kopi?
Is oat milk kopi available at Toast Box?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Oat Milk Kopi exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional food composition database. Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for brewed coffee and unsweetened oat milk in a ratio typical of Singapore kopitiam preparation. GI is estimated from analogous oat milk GI data in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and variability in sugar addition across vendors.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated composites; no direct laboratory analysis or primary database entry for Oat Milk Kopi was found. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero by formulation — plant-based oat milk contains no trans fats and robusta coffee contains no trans fats). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — oat milk is plant-based and contains no cholesterol; coffee contains no cholesterol). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — neither oat milk nor brewed coffee contributes meaningful vitamin C). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. glycemic_index is set to 39 (low GI) based on analogous oat milk GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is low. Sugar content reflects typical kopitiam sweetening; unsweetened versions will have lower sugar_g and lower glycemic load. Price data preserved from input: SGD 4.60 at Toast Box Funan.
